ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a double prawn/ebi burger feast combo meal. FOODON_00002473 (hamburger food product) is the closest defensible parent class. The feast format (burger + fries + beverage + pie) is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_description: Burger (hamburger type) — fast food composite meal
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FHR covers burger-type fast food items. No specific code exists for a double ebi/prawn burger feast combo. Approximate match used; feast components (fries, beverage, pie) are subsumed under the composite meal classification.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept maps specifically to a double prawn burger fast food feast. SNOMED CT food coding is not applicable at this level of specificity.

allergens: gluten (wheat bun, fries coating, pie pastry)
milk (possible in pie filling or bun)

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + medium fries + small Coke Zero + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard feast serving comprises the Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger, a medium portion of French fries, a small Coke Zero, and one baked pie, totalling approximately 570 g as sold.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger Feast — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values per 100 g are taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for the Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger Feast (cr_id G00010108). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are not reported in the HPB record; saturated fat is estimated from USDA Branded Food closest analogue (McDonald's Filet-O-Fish Meal, USDA FDC ID 2103965) and documented accordingly. Monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fat data are not available in either source and are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; supplemented by University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for white-bread-based burger bun and deep-fried potato (French fries)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific feast combo. GI estimated as a weighted composite: burger bun (white bread base, GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database), deep-fried French fries (GI ~75, University of Sydney GI Database), and Coke Zero (GI ~0, negligible carbohydrate contribution). The dominant carbohydrate contributors are the bun and fries. A blended GI of 72 is applied as a conservative high-GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100. Serving carbohydrate = 29.57 g/100g × 570 g serving = 168.5 g available carbohydrate; GL = (72 × 168.5) / 100 = 121.3, rounded to 120.9 after Coke Zero dilution adjustment.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (487 mg/100 g) with low potassium-to-sodium ratio (197 mg K vs 487 mg Na per 100 g); feast format compounds sodium load via salted fries and prawn patties
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal; sodium content per feast serving (~2776 mg) substantially exceeds the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg, and the low potassium-to-sodium ratio (0.40) provides minimal vascular offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~72) and very high glycaemic load per serving (~121) driven by white-flour bun, deep-fried high-GI French fries, and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This feast meal is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the combined glycaemic load of approximately 121 per serving will cause a sharp postprandial blood glucose spike, and the low fibre content (1.8 g/100 g) provides insufficient glycaemic buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Trans fat present (0.1 g/100 g), saturated fat ~2.8 g/100 g, high sodium (487 mg/100 g), deep-fried preparation generating oxidised lipids and AGEs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal; the presence of trans fat, elevated saturated fat, very high sodium per serving (~2776 mg), and deep-fried lipid oxidation products collectively elevate LDL cholesterol, promote endothelial inflammation, and increase blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation (double ebi patties and French fries) generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); trans fat present; nightshade ingredient (tomato sauce)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal entirely; deep-fried AGE-generating components are a hard disqualifier, trans fat is strongly pro-inflammatory, and tomato-based sauce introduces nightshade solanine which may exacerbate joint inflammation in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Double Ebi Burger Feast is a Singapore-market fast food combo meal featuring two deep-fried prawn (ebi) patties in a white-flour burger bun, topped with spicy tomato sauce, lettuce, and mayonnaise, served alongside a medium portion of deep-fried French fries, a small Coke Zero, and a baked pie. 'Ebi' is the Japanese word for prawn/shrimp, widely adopted in Singapore fast food branding. The meal is ultra-processed and deep-fried, yielding 263 kcal per 100 g with 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate. The feast format is high in sodium (487 mg/100 g, approximately 2776 mg per full serving), has a high glycaemic index (~72) and a very high glycaemic load per serving (~121), making it clinically unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. Trans fat is present at 0.1 g/100 g. The meal is halal-certified in the Singapore market context. It is typically consumed as a lunch or dinner meal and is positioned as a limited-time promotional feast set. Nutritional data sourced from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010108, confidence 0.95).

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values are sourced directly from the Singapore HPB Foodo database (cr_id G00010108) with a stated confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA Branded Food analogue and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for white-bread bun and deep-fried potato, which are the dominant carbohydrate contributors. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this specific feast combo are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA Branded Food as of 2026-04-16. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite feast meal is not reported in the HPB Foodo database or any available approved source. 3. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: micronutrient detail beyond the HPB-reported fields is not available in the HPB Foodo database for this record, and no approved secondary source provides a sufficiently close composite match for reliable estimation. 4. saturated_fat_g (2.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA Branded Food closest analogue (McDonald's Filet-O-Fish Meal, USDA FDC ID 2103965); this is an approximation and should be replaced with direct HPB or manufacturer data when available. 5. trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g) is carried forward from the input health_suitability clinical notes which reference trans fat presence; this value should be verified against the manufacturer's nutrition panel. 6. Glycaemic index of 72 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for white-bread-based burger bun (~72) and deep-fried French fries (~75); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific feast product. 7. Serving size of 570 g is an estimate based on typical Singapore fast food feast set weights; actual weight should be confirmed from the restaurant's published nutrition information. 8. is_halal set to true based on the Singapore market context for this product category; formal halal certification status should be verified with the restaurant operator.
